text: 40 years of Clar’s aromatic π-sextet rule In 1972, in a pioneering effort, the renowned organicschemist Erich Clar formulated his aromatic π-sextet rule in order to discuss in a qualitative way the aromatic character of the wide class of compounds known as “benzenoid”. For the last 40 years, the Clar’s rule has kept inspiring the chemists and its validity has been demonstrated at different levels (experimental and theoretical). This review is intended to show through specific examples, the large applicability of the Clar’s rule and also, to give a brief description of two recently reported cases where such rule is fully applicable.
